Super good! I didn’t go to this restaurant before it temporarily closed during covid, but apparently they were on the Michelin Guide. …

Really fresh and flavorful Ethiopian food! I remember the old Zoma location that closed during COVID so I was glad to see they reopened. We got the Zoma Tibs and Kitfo as well as lots of vegetarian sides. It was perfect for us as a group and the sambusas were freshly made!
Incredible food, and the kindest owner!! We tried a wide variety of their offerings and it was the best Ethiopian food we have all ever had

Excellent. So happy to find Zoma opened a few blocks from me.  I used to walk to it in Harlem. I had given up Ethiopian food because none of the restaurants nearby is good. Had an excellent meal. Owner and the chef are really nice. Space is …
Awesome food that’s also really healthy! The owner was so friendly and welcoming. Absolutely loved the experience and will definitely be coming back

The food was amazing, and the owner was so friendly. All the food was flavorful and authentic. I also enjoyed the cozy atmosphere. Definitely coming back and excited for this to be in my neighborhood.

Just got back from dinner at Zoma and let me tell you the reviews didn’t lie. The food is simply amazing and the atmosphere of the place is so cosy. The owners are very welcoming and friendly, will definitely be coming back soon!!
The food was amazing and service is top tier! Fitsum was great, he treated us so good he even gave us extra food to try for free. Highly recommend!

Delicious take out!strongly recommend the owners put themselves on yelp & Apple Maps as it was a little difficult to find the location but I’m so glad I did! The vegan combo was enough for two people and we were very impressed. Definitely coming back!
I found a gem in my neighborhood! I really love the vegetarian platter and fish I had. My 9 year old was excited to try Ethiopian and we both found the dishes so satisfying and delicious!

Zoma Exp was the best Ethiopia food I’ve tried in New York City, hands down! After the original location closed I couldn’t find anything of similar quality. I’m so happy they reopened! Really fresh, super high quality ingredients, and so flavorful. Zoma is a 10/10 for me!

Amazing place if you want to try good Ethiopian food! The staff was welcoming and very nice. Food was amazing and very good quality. Definitely would recommend checking this place out.

Great food at great prices! The dining-in experience is chill and cozy; the take-out travels well. Generous servings, delicious preparation.
So so yummy and great portions, highly recommend!

Amazing food. Fitz and his cousin run an incredibly authentic and delicious Ethiopian restaurant with wonderful music and service. The injera is 100% teff so gluten free and also 100% delicious! Highly recommend

Great place! First time here and first time trying Ethiopian food. Delicious. Plenty of flavor. The ladies here were so lovely, kind and welcoming! Wrapping up my meal with an Ethiopian coffee. Stop by and have lunch here it’s great!

The staff at this Ethiopian restaurant create a warm and welcoming experience, adding to the great quality food being served. 🇪🇹

The most delicious and authentic Ethiopian food you can find outside of Addis!! The owners are kind, welcoming and so hospitable. 10/10 food and service! Can’t wait to go back.

What a spectacular find! This lovely Ethiopian restaurant has a welcoming ambiance and delicious food cooked by sisters with complementary talents — one specializes in the vegetarian dishes and one in the meat dishes. Both are perfectly spiced and served with the care that such beautifully crafted food deserves.
First time trying Ethiopian food and the food here is absolutely delicious! Highly impressed by both the exquisite cuisine and the impeccable service.
A neighborhood gem - family run and always delicious! Highly recommend the vegetarian combo, awaze (lamb) tibs, doro wat, tomato salad, and baklava. The hot tea is excellent as well.
One of the best injera I've ever had! Everything was delicious, service was excellent and a very affordable price with a sweet ambience inside too! Highly recommend~

My walk through the area revealed this charming little spot. The staff adapted to our dietary needs and delivered quality service. Their dishes present flavors without comparison elsewhere. They serve big portions, charge fair prices, and make delicious meals. Enjoy and good luck!

This place is an absolute gem! Wow.

I can't help but smile when I think about eating here again. The interior feels high-class yet comfortable. I brought reinforcements for this meal. Capable workers who gave excellent suggestions and kept my family smiling the entire time. Nice performance! I'll be back!

An all around great experience. The food was great, and customer service was refreshing. A nice location easy to access 1 block away from the train station.

As a native Ethiopian, you’re not going to find better food anywhere in nyc than here. Trust me. Try the doro wot with a side of shiro wot and a Habesha beer. Tastes like home and smells like home too!

Charming, attentive, and delicious.  Those are the words that best describe this establishment.  The food tasted like it was made by someone that had invited me over for dinner and made just for the occasion.  I will be returning to this restaurant.

Delicious food, people working there are really nice, great vibe.

Highlight from the drinks menu: Ethiopian beer (which is delicious), and honey wine.

I now measure good food by what they serve here. The moment we entered, every employee showed exceptional kindness. The restaurant accommodates both casual diners and single eaters. They pride themselves on freshness above all. Pleasant employees, excellent meals, and a charming area.

I DON’T LIKE RAW TOMATOES. I gobbled it up. We also shared the Awaze Tibs to share. So spicy so delicious. 10/10 no notes!
The staff is very polite and attentive. They always made sure we had injera. The food was hearty, delicious and very filling.

My thoughts on this restaurant are belated. Their menu items feature impressive flavors. The restaurant exceeds typical expectations. Staff here work quickly while maintaining a positive attitude. I shared my approval of this spot.

Such delicious food. Fresh, flavorful ingredients. Staff were helpful with menu suggestions and very friendly. Will definitely be back!

Delicious and a lovely addition to the neighborhood! There's a gluten-free option for the injera and you can order it off menu, which is really convenient (compared to some other restaurants I've been to where you need to call ahead to request gf injera). Will be back.

Delicious food and a friendly atmosphere. We loved how bright and airy the space was. We got the tomato salad & veggie combo. Highly recommend!
This spot is a gem! Absolutely delicious food & lovely people. Their spiced tea is my new obsession, not gonna lie.

Lovely new spot in the neighborhood. So great to have delicious Ethiopian food in the Heights! We shared the vegetarian combo, the chickpea stew and chicken tibs. Fresh and flavorful! Very friendly service. We’ll be back!
AMAZING!! I had the ketanga and various dishes including doro wat and shiro and it was all wonderful! the servers and chef are all angels too! highley recommend

Simply outstanding food and service. The coffee and teas were excellent. The food was amazing. The deserts were outstanding too. The service was just heartwarming. Great value for your money to! The bean appetizer, chicken drumsticks and beef dishes were to die for. The carrot cake and rum soaked cake were divine!

Polite service, delicious food this was my first time having this dish and it was amazing! I would definitely come again. Just a bit pricey but worth it for a fresh meal that has flavor. Also the honey wine is the best wine I have had!!

Best experience having traditional Ethiopian food here everything was delicious. The service was great and I can’t wait to come back again. We got the injera and beef tibs + berber wings, they were to die for!

The owner is a very welcoming, polite, and hard working guy. He made sure our experience there was nothing short of exceptional. The injera was amazing! They have vegan options, as well. In photo: Beef Tibs, Shiro, and Alitcha

Great food - really nicely spiced. Friendly staff. Quiet and darkly lit; good spot for a date night.

My first time trying this cuisine ! So good! Food is amazing, same as place.

The food was delicious, the service was great, and the atmosphere is so welcoming and cool. …

Got the quesadilla and a meat + 2 vegan entree combination. You can’t go wrong with any of the dishes, honestly. Hearty, savory stews served with light, tangy injera!! And we got a rum raisin cake with coconut ice cream for dessert, naturally.

Great service and food, an exceptionally filling meal that’s perfect for sharing. As far as Ethiopian goes, you don’t get a ton of toppings, but you certainly won’t leave hungry. The guys that work here are great, and the neighborhood is lucky to have this spot.

Food was below average.  All the flavors were bland and everything we ordered tasted the same.  Injera was particularly soft and they had some nice red wines which is about as good as it got.   Service was fine but not overly attentive.

Amazing spot in Harlem!

Great service. Cute small place. The food is really good. The run soaked cake is strong! Beware about ordering it for a work lunch.

Blew me away. Fantastic food and great service. We stopped by for brunch. You could taste the love in the food. We started with the chicken sambusas & ful toast (not pictured here - we devoured it before remembering to take pics lol), …

There are not enough adjectives to describe the wonder of this food! I had an out of body experience...yep it was that amazing! Definitely a returning destination for me!! Service was steller.  Stopped in for a quick snack before a walk and returned for dinner! 5 stars are not enough!
A neighborhood gem! The food is excellent every time and the staff are such sweethearts. The restaurant itself is so cute and cozy. One of my favorite spots in the area!
Had a great lunch here today. It’s a casual spot but it has nice big windows with lots of light. …
Fantastic place. The ful is beautiful and the sharing vegan plate has all the Eritrean curries you want and lovely injera. Cool vibe and nice tea. Will be back!
My favorite restaurant in the area— the food is fantastic and the atmosphere is genuine and lovely. Abraham is wonderful.
What an incredible find! Food was delicious. Portions were huge. Tej (honey wine) was perfect. Ambience was great. And, best of all, Abraham was amazing - friendly and attentive.

I found this spot through a fortunate wrong turn. The warm setting keeps prices fair. The food maintains excellent standards. Cooperative, unpretentious, and sincere. I feel fortunate this spot is close by.

Exceptional food and excellent atmosphere! Abraham offers the best service! Best Ethiopian food in the city!

Great service and hospitality. I’ve eaten at a lot of Ethiopian spots and this was top notch and authentic. Portions were great. Wings also were very good.
Best coffee in the area. Try their Africano - it’s really good. The environment and the staff are very kind and friendly. I love this place.
